patient_data.chief_complaint: Widespread hives, throat tightness, and difficulty breathing within minutes of eating at a restaurant
symptoms: Generalized urticaria (hives) over trunk and extremities within 5 minutes
Throat tightness with hoarse voice (laryngeal edema)
Ate a dish at a restaurant likely cross-contaminated with peanuts
patient_data.history: Known severe peanut allergy (previous episode at age 12 — ED visit), carries EpiPen but left it at home today, mild asthma, allergic rhinitis

lab_results.tryptase: Not yet available (send urgently — draw within 1-2 hours)

should_detect: EPINEPHRINE IM 0.5mg (0.5mL of 1:1000) anterolateral thigh — STAT, repeat q5-15min if no improvement
Large-bore IV access x2 — aggressive fluid bolus NS 1-2L
High-flow oxygen — prepare for emergency intubation given stridor
Position: supine with legs elevated (NOT upright — risk of empty ventricle syndrome)
Adjuncts: IV diphenhydramine 50mg + IV ranitidine 50mg + IV methylprednisolone 125mg
Monitor for biphasic reaction — observe minimum 6-8 hours (longer given severity)
Serum tryptase within 1-2 hours (confirms mast cell degranulation)
Prescribe EpiPen 2-pack and allergy referral on discharge
red_flags: Known severe allergy with prior anaphylaxis
